title: The Billion Dollar Mistake 💸
description: Understand what Null is, and why it causes so many apps to crash.

slides.title: The Empty Box 📦
slides.content:
# What is Null?

Imagine you ask a friend: "What is your middle name?"
Your friend replies: "I don't have one."

In programming, when a variable represents the absence of a value—meaning it is completely hollow and empty—we say it is **`null`**.
It doesn't equal 0. It doesn't equal ''. It equals NOTHING.

slides.title: The Danger of Nothing 💣
slides.content:
## Null Reference Exceptions

The inventor of `null` called it his "Billion Dollar Mistake". Why?

Because if you try to use a method on a variable that is `null` (empty), your app instantly crashes!

`String middleName = null;`
`print(middleName.toUpperCase()); // BOOM! APP CRASH!`
You can't uppercase something that doesn't exist!

quizData.question: What happens if you try to access properties or methods (like `.length`) on a variable that is currently `null`?

options.text: The application immediately crashes with a Null Reference Exception.

quizData.explanation: Attempting to interact with an empty box as if it were a real object is the #1 cause of app crashes worldwide.

slides.title: Sound Null Safety 🛡️
slides.content:
## Dart's Shield

Older languages let you set anything to null by accident.
Dart introduced **Sound Null Safety**. This means Dart assumes EVERY variable MUST contain data, and explicitly forbids you from ever setting it to `null`!

```dart
// Dart compiler throws an error BEFORE the app even runs!
String name = null; // ERROR: A value of type 'Null' can't be assigned to a variable of type 'String'.
```
This prevents thousands of crashes!

quizData.question: Fill in the blank: By default, Dart's purely Sound ______ Safety prevents you from accidentally leaving a variable empty.
quizData.explanation: Sound Null Safety forces you to be intentional about creating empty variables.
